user_goal: Integrate the Founder Meeting v2, Hermes Watchdog, CAG/RAG, service-company, market-content-sales, and E1-E7 package into the ArchFlow public project.

cag_core_refs.purpose: public safety, live communication, approval, and task handout rules
E1-E7 spine and current task state
bounded RAG corpus and retrieval contract

rag_queries: current ArchFlow agentic stack and runtime boundaries
facts: Codex remains the executor, reviewer, file editor, validator, and final integration boundary.
Hermes is planned as watchdog/controller/reviewer, not executor.
Provider calls, external writeback, deploys, Notion mutation, Railway actions, Telegram sends, Linear mirrors, Figma sync, Git push, and production promotion require explicit owner approval.
The latest owner content instruction is 70% static educational or analytical posts, 20% carousel/checklist/template posts, and 10% short video or scripted demo posts.
interpretations: The package should be integrated additively into current architecture rather than copied over existing rules.
CAG/RAG should become a compact pre-execution context method, not a broad memory or ingestion system.
hypotheses: The first paid offer should be tested as a productized service before SaaS readiness is claimed.
Hermes can later supervise Codex and subagents through task contracts without becoming an executor.
gaps: No external actions are approved in this run.
Validated demand is not proven before E7 payment or firm paid-intent evidence.

role_assignment.scope: planned classification, task contract, review, and stop control
public safety, evidence, runtime boundary review

acceptance_criteria: Hermes is documented as watchdog/controller/reviewer only.
CAG/RAG schema, procedure, and run capsule exist.
Agent roster and skills governance distinguish shared and role-specific skills.
Service-company, market/content/sales, and E1-E7 packets are present.
No unsupported runtime, demand, ROI, or customer claim is promoted.
validation_checks: YAML parse for changed workflow/agent files
JSON parse for schema, capsule, and dashboard data
Python compile for changed Python files
stop_conditions: private or secret data would enter public files
external side effect is required without approval
broad unrelated refactor would be needed
